canonical_definition: Across the surveyed literature, LogicNLI is characterized as a benchmark for evaluating logical reasoning. It is consistently used to measure constructs like "multi-step logical reasoning" and, more specifically, "first-order logic reasoning". The benchmark is structured as an NLI-style dataset, defined as a task of "hypothesis verification from facts and rules". A frequently mentioned design feature is its attempt to "isolate first-order logic reasoning from commonsense inference," which one paper suggests allows for a "precise logical evaluation." Containing 20k examples according to one source, it is often used in evaluations alongside other logical reasoning datasets such as RobustLR, ProofWriter, and LOGIQA.
papers: Extracting Linguistic Information from Large Language Models: Syntactic Relations and Derivational Knowledge
SOLAR: Towards Characterizing Subjectivity of Individuals through Modeling Value Conflicts and Trade-offs
SemVink: AdvancingVLMs’ Semantic Understanding of Optical Illusions via Visual Global Thinking

definitions.text: A logical reasoning benchmark used to assess hypothesis verification from facts and rules.
definitions.paper: SOLAR: Towards Characterizing Subjectivity of Individuals through Modeling Value Conflicts and Trade-offs

relationships.paper: MAPS: Motivation-Aware Personalized Search viaLLM-Driven Consultation Alignment
relationships.evidence: We evaluate our framework on five multi-step logical reasoning datasets: RobustLR (Sanyal et al., 2022), PrOntoQA-OOD (Saparov et al., 2023), ProofWriter (Tafjord et al., 2020), ParaRules (Clark et al., 2020), LogicNLI (Tian et al., 2021) (Section 4.1).
